meaning: n.用力拖拉,拖,拉,捕获物,努力得到的结果,(尤指)一网捕获的鱼量,拖运距离vi.拖,拉,改变方向,改变主意vt.拖拉,拖运
examples: A crane had to be used to haul the car out of the stream.
Passengers on long-haul flights are being warned about the risks of deep vein thrombosis.
They grab Jake and haul him to his feet.
And Stuart Broad marked his call-up to the Test squad with a five-wicket haul.
By contrast, I found the sight of a haul of smuggled tusks in Bangkok Airport profoundly depressing.
